sourceMovie.title: A Million Ways to Die in the West
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit A Million Ways to Die in the West for the same reason it broke through in 2014: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a western rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Seth MacFarlane keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and western blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: ¡Three Amigos!
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Million Ways to Die in the West clicked for you, ¡Three Amigos! (1986) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and western, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after A Million Ways to Die in the West?
faq.answer: Try ¡Three Amigos!, City Slickers, Moana—each shares DNA with A Million Ways to Die in the West while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is A Million Ways to Die in the West?
faq.answer: A Million Ways to Die in the West (2014) is best described as Comedy, Western. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is A Million Ways to Die in the West worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
